questions.q: In which year was the Battle of Plassey fought?
questions.explanation: The Battle of Plassey was fought in 1757 CE.

questions.q: The Battle of Plassey was fought between the British East India Company and the Nawab of which province?
questions.explanation: The battle pitted the Company against Siraj-ud-Daulah, the Nawab of Bengal.

questions.q: Which British commander led the East India Company's forces at Plassey?
questions.explanation: Robert Clive commanded the Company's army at Plassey.

questions.q: Who was the Nawab of Bengal defeated at the Battle of Plassey?
questions.explanation: Siraj-ud-Daulah, the Nawab of Bengal, was defeated at Plassey.

questions.q: The defection of which of the Nawab's commanders was central to the British victory?
questions.explanation: The battle was won largely through the defection of the Nawab's commander Mir Jafar.

questions.q: What did the British gain control of as a result of their victory at Plassey?
questions.explanation: Victory gave the Company control of Bengal's wealth and resources.

questions.q: Plassey is often described as a decisive step in the rise of what in India?
questions.explanation: Plassey marked a decisive step in the East India Company's rise to political power in India.

questions.q: In which present-day Indian state did the Battle of Plassey take place?
questions.explanation: Plassey (Palashi) lies in present-day West Bengal.

questions.q: Who was rewarded with the throne of Bengal by the British after Plassey?
questions.explanation: Mir Jafar was installed as the new Nawab of Bengal after his betrayal helped win the battle.

questions.q: Plassey is the anglicised name of which place?
questions.explanation: Plassey is the British rendering of Palashi, named for the palash trees of the area.

questions.q: On what date was the Battle of Plassey fought?
questions.explanation: The battle took place on 23 June 1757.

questions.q: On the banks of which river was the Battle of Plassey fought?
questions.explanation: Plassey lay on the Bhagirathi-Hooghly river between Calcutta and Murshidabad.

questions.q: How did the size of the Nawab's army compare with Clive's force at Plassey?
options: The Nawab's army was far larger
The armies were equal in size
questions.explanation: Siraj's tens of thousands vastly outnumbered Clive's few thousand, but treachery decided the result.

questions.q: Which loyal commander of the Nawab was mortally wounded early, demoralising the Bengal army?
questions.explanation: The death of the loyal Mir Madan threw the Nawab's army into confusion.

questions.q: A sudden monsoon downpour during the battle damaged whose gunpowder?
options: The British, while the Nawab covered his
The Nawab's, while the British covered theirs
questions.explanation: Rain spoiled the Nawab's exposed powder while the British kept theirs dry, weakening Siraj's artillery.

questions.q: Which wealthy banking house is widely said to have helped finance the conspiracy behind Plassey?
questions.explanation: The Jagat Seth bankers of Murshidabad backed the plot against Siraj-ud-Daulah.

questions.q: Which French settlement had Clive captured shortly before Plassey, removing a European ally of the Nawab?
questions.explanation: The British seized the French post of Chandernagore before marching on Plassey.

questions.q: About how long did the actual fighting at Plassey effectively last?
questions.explanation: Plassey was decided in the course of a single day, largely by defection rather than prolonged combat.

questions.q: Which Mughal emperor reigned, in name only, at the time of Plassey?
questions.explanation: Alamgir II was the nominal Mughal emperor in 1757, the empire by then much weakened.

questions.q: What happened to Siraj-ud-Daulah after his defeat at Plassey?
options: He fled, was captured, and was killed
questions.explanation: Siraj fled, was captured, and was executed by the victorious faction soon after the battle.

questions.q: Plassey lay between the Bengal capital and which Company stronghold that Clive marched from?
questions.explanation: Clive advanced from the recaptured Company base at Calcutta toward Murshidabad, fighting at Plassey en route.

questions.q: Which mango grove gave the battlefield of Plassey its characteristic cover for Clive's troops?
questions.explanation: Clive's men took position in and around a large mango grove (Laksha Bagh) at Palashi.

questions.q: Besides Mir Jafar, which two of Siraj's generals also stood aside or held back their troops at Plassey?
options: Rai Durlabh and Yar Lutuf Khan
questions.explanation: Rai Durlabh and Yar Lutuf Khan, like Mir Jafar, kept their forces idle, dooming the Nawab.

questions.q: The secret agreement that arranged Mir Jafar's betrayal was negotiated partly through which Company agent and merchant intermediary?
options: Vasco da Gama and the Zamorin
questions.explanation: William Watts handled the secret treaty, with the merchant Omichund (Amir Chand) entangled in the intrigue.

questions.q: The famous deception in which Clive used a fake 'red' treaty to outwit a greedy intermediary involved whom?
questions.explanation: Clive prepared a false red treaty to cheat Omichund of the share he had demanded for his role in the plot.

questions.q: Mir Jafar's reign as British-backed Nawab soon gave way to that of which successor, his own son-in-law?
questions.explanation: Mir Jafar was later replaced by Mir Qasim, his son-in-law, in the unstable post-Plassey years.

questions.q: The larger 1764 battle that more firmly cemented Company power after Plassey was fought at which place?
questions.explanation: The Battle of Buxar in 1764 decisively confirmed and broadened the Company's dominance won at Plassey.

questions.q: Following Plassey, the Company extracted the right to collect revenue (diwani) of Bengal, formally granted in which year?
questions.explanation: The Diwani of Bengal, Bihar, and Orissa was granted to the Company by the Mughal emperor in 1765, after Buxar.

questions.q: Robert Clive's enrichment after Plassey, including a large jagir, later drew what kind of scrutiny in Britain?
options: A parliamentary inquiry into his conduct and wealth
questions.explanation: Clive's vast post-Plassey fortune led to parliamentary inquiry into his conduct in Bengal.

questions.q: Historians often regard Plassey as significant less for its military scale and more for what?
options: Demonstrating that intrigue and bribery could topple an Indian state
Its use of new artillery technology
questions.explanation: Plassey is remembered chiefly because conspiracy and bribery, not battlefield prowess, delivered Bengal to the Company.
